summary: Safety limitations are complete and non-blocking for this provenance repair. The adverse-event outcome and result remain bounded to posted ClinicalTrials.gov aggregate group counts, cite the retained adverse-events section, and do not create event-specific comparative safety effects beyond the retained registry text.

findings.claim_or_issue: The safety outcome and result cite section:adverse-events and retain aggregate adverse-event wording and counts rather than creating event-specific safety result records.
findings.why_it_matters: Posted registry aggregate counts are useful for evidence mapping but are not enough to support preferred-term comparative safety claims.
findings.recommended_action: Keep event-specific adverse-event extraction as a separate future extraction-refresh task.

findings.claim_or_issue: The safety result states aggregate serious, death, and other-adverse-event counts and includes caveats that preferred-term enumeration and formal safety synthesis remain pending.
findings.why_it_matters: This preserves the boundary between registry aggregate reporting and event-specific comparative safety interpretation.
findings.recommended_action: Do not promote any event-specific safety inference until preferred-term counts and comparative effect fields are extracted and reviewed.
